title: Luxury Commerce and Consumer Platforms — Leo Burnett/Publicis, Singapore
summary: Luxury e-commerce, consumer-platform, CMS, and activation work across the USA and Asia Pacific.
body:
Luxury commerce and consumer-platform work at Leo Burnett/Publicis covers SK-II, Samsung, and Skyscanner across the USA and Asia Pacific.

Included projects
- SK-II USA e-commerce CMS
- SK-II Asia Pacific CMS
- Samsung Digital Appliances CMS
- Skyscanner Activation

Strategic and technical themes
- Luxury and consumer commerce experience design.
- CMS and platform work across regional and multi-market delivery contexts.
- Agency-side product and workflow collaboration for faster delivery.
- Activation and campaign work that still required platform thinking, not only surface design.

Proof connection
- This programme can reference the existing 70% handoff-friction reduction proof from Leo Burnett/Publicis.
- It must not attribute that metric to every listed client project.

Retrieval guidance
- Use this record for Leo Burnett, Publicis, SK-II, Samsung, Skyscanner, luxury-commerce, or commerce-platform questions.

claims: Joz worked on luxury commerce, CMS, and consumer-platform initiatives at Leo Burnett/Publicis.
The programme included SK-II in the USA and APAC, Samsung digital-appliances CMS work, and Skyscanner activation.
proof_points: The broader Leo Burnett/Publicis proof includes a 70% reduction in handoff friction through code-based prototyping.
This record narrows retrieval to the grouped commerce and consumer platform programme.
metadata.source_notes: Grouped programme record. The 70% handoff-friction metric belongs to the broader Publicis delivery environment, not every individual client project.
